title: School Nurse
subtitle: PreK-8 Student Support Services
salaryRange: Starting salary begins at $54,500, with final compensation structured in accordance with the current Collective Bargaining Agreement.
roleDescription: The School Nurse supports student attendance and success by managing health office operations, maximizing overall building wellness, delivering localized clinical care, and acting as a dependable resource for families, staff, and community health networks.
roleHighlight: Specialized Framework Assignment: This position requires an expansive familiarity with the student IEP and Section 504 framework to effectively assess and support the direct impact of medical factors on student learning configurations.
responsibilities: Provide first aid, initial triage care, appropriate parent notification, and medical referrals
Administer medications and treatments and perform specialized health care procedures and support (e.g., toileting, etc.)
Provide designated case management for student 504 Plans as appropriate within the building framework
Complete hearing and vision screenings alongside comprehensive health histories for student evaluations under IDEA
Execute targeted hearing and vision screenings for district Play-Based Assessments and Preschool Screening events
Assess individual health needs and develop, implement, and collaborate on student care plans
Ensure compliance with State of Illinois mandates for physical examinations, images, immunizations, and medical reports
Manage communicable diseases in accordance with Skokie and Illinois Department of Public Health guidelines
Oversee the District 73 Vision and Hearing Screening Program, handling referrals and timely state reporting
Access community resources and develop partnerships to improve health and wellness access for the D73 community
Monitor emerging public health concerns (e.g., infectious diseases) and advise the district on mitigation plans
Assist with main office administrative duties and front desk coverage as needed to support building operations
Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the health office
requiredQuals: Valid Illinois Registered Professional Nurse (RN) License
Comprehensive understanding of the operational needs of school health offices
Familiarity with the IEP and Section 504 framework
Knowledge in pediatrics, public health, mental health, and education frameworks
preferredQuals: Excellent technology skills, including proficiency with Google Suite
Strong interpersonal skills with a demonstrated ability to work collaboratively with colleagues, community, and staff
Demonstrated ability to work independently with minimal supervision while meeting time constraints
Maintains professional boundaries and consistently demonstrates ethical behavior
benefits: Medical, Dental, and Life Insurance Plans
Illinois Municipal Retirement Fund (IMRF) Pension Framework Participation
Optional Employee Contribution Access to 403(b) and 457 Retirement Accounts
Fixed Annual Allotment of 15 Sick Days and 3 Personal Days
cultureText: As the sole medical professional in the building, physical demands include regular standing, walking, typing with frequent interruptions, meeting strict deadlines, and occasionally lifting or moving up to 30 pounds.
matchQuestions: Do you hold a current RN license and a valid CPR/AED/First Aid certification?
Are you confident managing Illinois state mandates for immunizations, vision/hearing screenings, and state health reports?
Are you comfortable working independently as a building's sole nurse while occasionally assisting with main office workflows?
Can you work efficiently in an environment with frequent interruptions and strict timelines?
